title: 1x12 Cubby Bench
summary: A small three-cubby entryway bench built entirely from 1x12 and 1x3 boards, with rounded top corners and a footer/back trim board — sized to hold fabric storage bins.
description:
This bench is a simple 1x12 box with a footprint sized around three fabric storage bins rather than a bespoke cubby size, which makes it a fast, low-waste build for anyone who already owns or plans to buy bins in that common 11" size. Round off the top corners of the two side pieces with a jigsaw first, clamping both cut edges together to sand them into a matching curve — doing this before assembly means you're sanding two loose boards on a bench, not an assembled box.

Attach the shelves to the sides next, then the front and back footer boards — **for the bottom footer piece, fasten it from both directions**, into the shelf from below and into the footer's top edge, since that joint carries the bench's seated weight. The back panel is genuinely optional; it's nailed on last with finish nails and glue, and the plan notes more shelves get added afterward specifically to give the back panel extra places to grab onto.

The cubby dividers go in after the back, marked, predrilled, and screwed down — a straightforward step. The final top dividers rely on glue more than mechanical fasteners: measure their placement, apply glue, slide them in, then screw down through the seat from above for extra hold while the glue cures.

tools.note: Rounding the top corners of the sides.

materials.name: Wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

cutList.note: Top corners rounded with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Round the side corners
steps.body: Round off the top corners of the two side pieces with a jigsaw. Clamp both cut edges together and sand as one to match the curve.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body: Mark the sides for shelf placement, predrill, and attach the shelves to the sides with 2" screws and glue, or pocket hole screws and glue.
materials: Wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Attach the footer/back boards
steps.body: Attach the front and back footer boards. For the bottom piece, also fasten from the top down into the footer's top edge.
materials: Wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Attach the back panel (optional)
steps.body: Attach the back panel with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue. Add extra fasteners at the top; clamp in place if additional support is needed while the glue dries.

steps.title: Add the cubby dividers
steps.body: Mark, place, and predrill the cubby dividers, then screw down.
materials: Wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Add the top dividers
steps.body: Measure and mark the placement of the final top dividers, apply glue, and slide into place. Screw down through the seat for extra hold and let dry fully.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill any screw holes with wood filler, sand all surfaces, and apply primer and paint. Add fabric storage bins.

images.alt: Small three-cubby entryway bench with fabric bins
